data.narrative:
Teradar is a Series B defense and security company founded by MIT-trained CEO Matt Carey, with CTO Gregory Charvat (ex-CTO of Humatics) and Chief Chip Architect Nick Saiz leading terahertz IC development. The peer universe comprises 8 same-sector, same-stage private companies—including Vannevar Labs, Dream Security, Harmattan AI, Code Metal, and Castelion—plus 2 public comparables (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS).

Two valuation methods anchor the analysis. Stage-progression, using the company's $175M total raised as a proxy, applies a 1.9x Series B step-up multiplier, implying $332M. Peer-median triangulation across the 8-company comp set yields $1.1B, with a range spanning $300M to $1.5B. The stage-progression method reflects a conservative floor anchored to actual capital deployed; the peer median reflects market pricing for functionally comparable late-stage frontier-tech defense plays.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $283M (low), $729M (median), to $1.3B (high). The wide dispersion reflects genuine uncertainty: the peer set shows substantial valuation heterogeneity, and stage-progression and peer methods diverge materially. Key uncertainties include customer concentration risk in defense procurement, technology differentiation relative to peers on core terahertz applications, and contract backlog visibility.
